category_title: Children who exhibit aggressive interpersonal behaviors
minimal_context: The student is expected to join their classmates in a circle on the rug for a group teaching lesson.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for our group teaching lesson. Everyone needs to sit in the circle on the rug.

STUDENT: (Stands at the edge of the room, not participating in the group activity and showing a lack of attention.)

TUTOR: Please come over here and join the class activity.

STUDENT: (Leaves the area without permission and starts running aimlessly around the classroom, disturbing the group activity.)

TUTOR: Stop running. You need to follow the rules and sit next to your classmates.

STUDENT: No! (Runs into the circle, pushes another child, and displays resistance by refusing to stop the aggressive behavior.)

options.A: Alter the original lesson plan to incorporate the student's contributions.
options.B: Intensify efforts to aid the student's emotional regulation.
options.C: Provide verbal reinforcement when the student exhibits the replacement behavior.
options.D: Ask open-ended questions about the group teaching lesson.
